seoTitle: Lefroy vs Legana — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Lefroy and Legana. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lefroy and Legana.
heading: Lefroy vs Legana — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lefroy, Tasmania and Legana, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lefroy has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Legana (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lefroy or Legana safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lefroy has a flood risk score of 38/100, while Legana scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lefroy scores 51/100 for bushfire risk. Legana scores 52/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lefroy or Legana?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lefroy has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.93% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
